2026-04-20T10:52:42.9872253Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9872814Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9873246Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9873619Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9873933Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9874187Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9874361Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9874556Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9874734Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9874975Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9875185Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9875475Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T10:52:42.9875735Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-20T10:55:51.2042791Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T10:53:50.343137093Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58380","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-20T10:55:51.2072979Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T10:53:50.713147827Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58380,\"title\":\"[AI Agent Server] Docs: README missing chat isolation, session ID header, metadata constraints, and updated validation behavior\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 70b48842 (PR #58371) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` 1.0.0-beta.3 was released (triggered by the version-increment commit). The 1.0.0-beta.2 and 1.0.0-beta.3 releases added several user-facing behaviors — chat isolation enforcement, session ID header, metadata validation, and `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation — that are not documented in the README or troubleshooting section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. This is a multi-tenancy security feature (added beta.2): when a response is created with this header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls must include the same key; mismatched or missing keys return 404. Users implementing multi-tenant servers need to be aware of this behavior.\\n- **`x-agent-session-id` response header** (added beta.2) is not documented. The library echoes the resolved session ID as a response header on all protocol endpoints; clients may need to forward or log this header.\\n- **Metadata constraints** (added beta.2) are not documented: metadata maps are limited to 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 characters, value length ≤ 512 characters. Users passing metadata will receive 400 errors without knowing the limits unless they read the CHANGELOG.\\n- **`previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` validation** (added beta.3): these values are now validated against the provider before the handler is invoked, returning 404/400 immediately. The Troubleshooting section does not list these new error cases.\\n- **Troubleshooting section** is missing error entries for: isolation key mismatch → 404, invalid `previous_response_id` → 404, invalid `conversation.id` → 400, metadata constraint violation → 400.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n**1a. Add a \\u0026#34;Chat isolation\\u0026#34; subsection under `## Key concepts`** (after the \\u0026#34;Thread safety\\u0026#34; section, before `## Examples`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nTo enforce tenant isolation, clients may include an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header when creating a response. Once set, every subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems call for that response **must** include the same key. Calls with a mismatched or missing key return **404** (indistinguishable from not-found) to prevent cross-tenant information leakage.\\n\\nThe isolation key value is never logged.\\n\\n### Session ID header\\n\\nAll protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, InputItems) echo the resolved session ID as the `x-agent-session-id` response header. The value corresponds to the `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable (or the deterministically derived session ID when a conversational context is available). Clients that need to correlate requests to a session can read this header from any response.\\n\\n### Metadata constraints\\n\\nMetadata maps supplied in the request body are validated before the handler is invoked:\\n- Maximum **16 keys**\\n- Key length ≤ **64 characters**\\n- Value length ≤ **512 characters**\\n\\nRequests that exceed these limits are rejected with **400 Bad Request** before your handler is called.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Expand the `### Common errors` subsection under `## Troubleshooting`** to add new entries:\\n\\nAdd the following bullets to the existing list:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (conversation validation): `previous_response_id` was supplied but does not exist in the provider. Validation happens before your handler is invoked; the resolved history is cached for the request lifetime.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (conversation validation): `conversation.id` was supplied but does not match the stored conversation record.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (metadata): A metadata map exceeds 16 keys, or a key exceeds 64 characters, or a value exceeds 512 characters.\\n- **404 Not Found** (isolation key): A GET, Cancel, DELETE, or InputItems request was made with a missing or mismatched `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` relative to the value used at creation time.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for this change. The documentation additions are conceptual/reference prose, not runnable sample code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already has all required sections (Getting started → Key concepts → Examples → Troubleshooting → Next steps → Contributing). The edits above add subsections inside existing sections; no structural reorganization is needed.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm git diff shows no unintended changes to README snippet blocks\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected; verify the API listing is unchanged\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24661992977/agentic_workflow) · ● 388.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58380\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T10:51:08Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T10:51:08Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The 1.0.0-beta.2 and 1.0.0-beta.3 releases added several user-facing behaviors — chat isolation enforcement, session ID header, metadata validation, and `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id` validation — that are not documented in the README or troubleshooting section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README. 2026-04-20T10:55:51.2253190Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T10:54:25.394993954Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":130,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
